title: Records/Licenses/Permits Fee Payment Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Records/Licenses/Permits Fee Payment Assistance 31 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Transgender Individuals Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES EUGENE CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with clothing, personal hygiene products, diapers, and showers. Helps connect individuals with other community resources and access mainstream benefits, including assistance with SNAP applications and local transit single-ride bus passes (which can be requested twice per month, subject to availability). Also, assists in completing forms to obtain ID and birth certificates, including limited payment assistance. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and other items. 1464 W 6th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-4pm, Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with clothing, personal hygiene products, diapers, and showers. Helps connect individuals with other community resources and access mainstream benefits, including assistance with SNAP applications and local transit single-ride bus passes (which can be requested twice per month, subject to availability). Also, assists in completing forms to obtain ID and birth certificates, including limited payment assistance. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and other items. 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-4pm, Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry vouchers, assistance with water bills, gently used clothing, hygiene items, bus passes, and financial assistance in getting state identificaton cards. 1440 Birch Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Individuals who self-identify as being low-income Hours: Monday 1:30pm-6pm, Tuesday-Friday 11am-3:30pm Email (541) 942-2176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMPASS OUTREACH NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Partners with local churches to provide hot lunches and a bi-monthly medical clinic. Other services include case management, hygiene supplies, temporary mailing address, and phone messaging. Replacement ID services are available in exchange for volunteer service. Tents, tarps, and blankets offered when available and in exchange for volunteer service (can be waived if needed). Bus passes are available for court or parole meetings in Coquille. Local bus passes are available on a case-by-case basis. 1200 Newmark Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: -Unrestricted; priority is given to people experiencing homelessness with mental health conditions -Bus passes: Local transportation to Coquille for court or parole meetings. Must show summons or ticket. -Local bus passes on a case-by-case basis -Volunteer time may be requested for high-demand or limited-availability items, on a case-by-case basis. Hours: Monday/Wednesday 11am-12:30pm at Gloria Dei Lutheran Church, 1290 Thompson Road Coos Bay, OR 97240 Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 11am-12:30pm at Harmony Methodist Church, 123 Ocean Boulevard SE, Coos Bay, OR 97420 Email (541) 888-3202 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONGREGATIONS HELPING PEOPLE BIRTH CERTIFICATE AND OREGON ID CARD ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers payment assistance to obtain birth certificates or Oregon State identification. No assistance is available for expired ID cards. Assistance is limited to one time. 1977 Front Street NE Salem, OR 97303 Eligibility: Oregon ID: Individuals who are new to the state of Oregon or need a valid ID replaced. Birth certificate: Individuals with a verifiable business need Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 391-4365 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORVALLIS DAYTIME DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Drop-in center providing a space to socialize, have coffee and snacks, receive mail, phone charging, employment support, information from local health and social organizations, and counseling. Provides assistance with acquiring clothing and shoes; personal hygiene items; medical and dental fees/services; and can assist with transportation needs. Also offers navigation services such as applying for SNAP; completing, obtaining, or replacing official documents like driver's license and birth certificates; access to health providers; housing assistance wait lists; and obtaining a GED. 530 SW 4th Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Homeless and low-income individuals and families; individuals with disabilities Hours: Basic needs: Monday-Friday 9am-noon Social Services support: Monday-Friday noon-2pm by appointment Email (458) 233-5327 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DETERMINATION OF HOMELESSNESS AGENCY LIST AGENCIES WHICH CAN PROVIDE DETERMINATION OF HOMELESSNESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The proof of homelessness document allows people to access state and local programs geared to help the homeless population. Examples include certain housing programs, student loans, OHP, and no-cost Oregon identification cards. The following is a list of agencies that callers can contact to receive a Determination of Homelessness Form: Clackamas County: Clackamas Service Center Kaiser Permanente NW Impact The Father’s Heart Street Ministry Providence (BOB) Better Outcomes through Bridges Program Multnomah County: Alano Club Portland All Good NW Cascadia Behavioral Healthcare Catholic Charities Central City Concern Department of Community Justice Dignity Village Do Good Multnomah Fora Health Treatment and Recovery Gresham Women’s Shelter Immigration and Refugee Community Organization (IRCO) JOIN Kaiser Permanente Lutheran Community Services Northwest The Miracles Club Multnomah County Health Department NARA NW Family Services NW Impact Northwest Pilot Project OHSU Outside In Oregon Law Center Operation Nightwatch Pathfinder Network-The Center for Family Services PDX Saints Love Portland Rescue Mission Providence (BOB) Better Outcomes through Bridges Program Right 2 Dream Too Rose Haven Self Enhancement Inc Transition Projects (TPI) Urban League of Portland Volunteers of America Men’s Residential Center YWCA of Greater Portland Washington County: Boys and Girls Aid Community Action Family Promise of Greater Washington County Just Compassion Kaiser Permanente Morrison Child and Family Services New Narrative Open Door Housing Works Project Homeless Connect Confidential Address Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by site More Details FISH OF ALBANY INC BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, clothing, shoes, bedding, personal and feminine hygiene products, diapers, baby food and formula, assistance in acquiring identification documents, gasoline and propane vouchers, and local bus tickets. Availability is subject to donations and eligibility. 1035 SE 2nd Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Food and clothing: By appointment only All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 928-4460 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF LEBANON BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, utility assistance, heating fuel assistance, prescription assistance, and clothing. Also offers financial assistance to obtain a valid ID card or driver's license through the Oregon Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV). Assistance is available once per year for each service. 145 W Ash Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 259-3200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOODWILL INDUSTRIES OF LANE AND SOUTH COAST COUNTIES GOODWILL WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT VETERAN CONNECTIONS HOMELESS VETERAN REINTEGRATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment-related services to reintegrate homeless veterans into the labor force. Services include job search training and placement, employment readiness training, career exploration, and vocational/technical training. Also provides information about community resources, including housing, transportation, identification, clothing, computer access, and more. 855 Seneca Road Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: -Must be homeless or at risk of becoming homeless, or have been homeless within 60 days prior to enrollment -Must have served at least one day of active military service and have been discharged under any status other than dishonorable Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 431-3339 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ILLINOIS VALLEY FAMILY COALITION BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an emergency food pantry, clothing vouchers, laundry vouchers, shower vouchers, a hygiene closet, assistance with identification cards and birth certificates, and bus passes with transportation to job interviews. 535 E River Street Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 592-6139 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides street outreach and individual case management for youth and young adults. Offers support, case management, and goal-setting tools to help identify and work towards future goals. Financial help and referrals to obtain interview clothes, laundry, obtain a birth certificate, Social Security card, ID, CPR/First Aid, or Food Handlers certification. Family mediation is available for families needing to work through a specific conflict. 225 SW 4th Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Ages 10-24. Individuals from outside the service area will not be turned away. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-7pm, Saturday-Sunday by appointment Email (800) 901-2904 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES YOUTH SERVICE CENTER AND DROP IN OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center with street outreach and individual case management. Offers support, resources, referral help, and physical items. Case management and goal setting can be set up to help an individual name and work towards future goals. Family mediation is also available for families needing to work through a specific conflict. Financial help and referrals for youth needing to do laundry, obtain interview clothes, or obtain a birth certificate, Social Security card, ID, CPR/First Aid, or Food Handlers certification is also available. 1025 Pacific Boulevard SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Ages 10-24. Individuals from outside the service area will not be turned away. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-7pm, Saturday-Sunday by appointment Email (800) 901-2904 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOWER COLUMBIA Q CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources and support services to the LGBTQ2SIA+ community and allies of the Lower Columbia region. Services include a community center, education opportunities, mentorship, and monthly support groups for adults, youth, and families. Limited financial assistance is available as funds allow for gender-affirming costs, name changes, hormone treatments, and other expenses approved on a case-by-case basis. Financial assistance is limited to once per year, with a $200 limit. 171 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Community Center walk in hours: Tuesday 2pm-5pm, Thursday noon-4pm. Other hours vary by service. Email (503) 468-5155 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID ROGUE FOUNDATION MOBILE NAVIGATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outreach assistance with housing and job search help, identification assistance (ID and Social Security cards), replacement birth certificate help with application and fee, general mail delivery, credit report requests, and SNAP and OHP application assistance. Also helps to connect people to services for savings, spending, educational plans, and life skills. Mobile Navigators meet clients in public spaces that work best for the client. PO Box 1972 Grants Pass, OR 97528 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 295-5706 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ONE COMMUNITY HEALTH HOOD RIVER CLINIC LGBTQ2+ HEALTH CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of services specifically for the LGBTQ2+ community, including gender-affirming care (initiation and maintenance of hormone therapy and referrals to gender-affirming care specialists), behavioral health providers with specialized training in LGBTQ2+ care, assistance with legal ID changes through the county, DMV, and passports, reproductive health care and STI screening. 849 Pacific Avenue Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Varies by service, established One Community Health patients only Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm, Saturday 8am-3pm On-call provider and nurse: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 386-6380 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ONE COMMUNITY HEALTH THE DALLES CLINIC LGBTQ2+ HEALTH CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of services specifically for the LGBTQ2+ community, including gender-affirming care (initiation and maintenance of hormone therapy and referrals to gender-affirming care specialists), behavioral health providers with specialized training in LGBTQ2+ care, assistance with legal ID changes through the county, DMV, and passports, reproductive health care and STI screening. 1040 Webber Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Varies by service, established One Community Health patients only Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm, Saturday 8am-3pm On-call provider and nurse: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 296-4610 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES IDENTIFICATION CARD FEE WAIVER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Oregon State Legislature passed HB3026 on June 11, 2021, which directs the Department of Transportation to waive the fees for issuing, renewing, or replacing an identification card for a person experiencing homelessness. Fee will be waived for one, original ID card and up to two replacement cards during the 8 year valid through date. 1905 Lana Avenue NE Salem, OR 97314 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Varies by location (503) 945-5000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON WORKER RELIEF EQUITY CORPS OF OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides immigration legal services including access to lawyers, legal clinics, financial assistance with filing fees, and general information and instruction about immigration law and immigrant rights. Eligibility: Household must be below 200% of the federal poverty level and at risk of deportation or need help with an immigration legal matter Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm Email (888) 274-7292 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN ID PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with changing name/gender through the courts or at the DMV (cannot do both for one person). Services range from providing information on the process of changing documents in the state of Oregon to offering financial aid and advocacy at the courts and the DMV. The level of financial assistance and eligibility for this assistance varies based on program's budget. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: People who identify as transgender or gender-nonconforming Hours: By appointment only Email (503) 535-3828 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PDX SAINTS LOVE DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center for sheltering, hot meals, haircuts, clothing, public restrooms, bus passes, wound care, hygiene kits, OHP and SNAP form assistance, ID and birth record replacement and payment assistance, resources for housing navigation and shelter referrals, detox referrals, overdose prevention, and Narcan supplies. Community Health Workers (CHWs), Peer Wellness Specialists (PWS), and Peer Support Specialists (PSS) are available to assist with resources, connections to health care, and case management. 247 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Day Center: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm, Thanksgiving and Christmas 9am-noon Hot meals: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Haircuts: Monday 10am-2pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY RESOURCE CENTERS MONMOUTH RESOURCE CENTER RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource drop-in center with the following services: comprehensive information and referral, food boxes, a proof of homelessness document for a free Oregon identification card, prescription expense assistance, hygiene items, diapers, formula, transportation passes, and laundry passes. Assistance is based on available funding and is not guaranteed. 1407 Monmouth Independence Highway Monmouth, OR 97361 Eligibility: Unrestricted Transportation passes: Once per month for medical appointments, job interviews, or other appointments supported by documentation Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 751-1644 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROJECT HOMELESS CONNECT IDENTIFICATION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a Determination of Homelessness form needed to apply for an ID Card from the Oregon Driver & Motor Vehicle Services (DMV). The agency does not provide driver’s licenses or birth certificates. 1440 SE 21st Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Wednesday-Sunday 9am-5pm Email (971) 713-3930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOCIETY OF ST VINCENT DE PAUL SOCIAL SERVICES OF BEND ID AND DRIVERS LICENSE ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with the cost of renewing, replacing, or original application for ID's and Drivers Licence. This does not include reinstatement fees, the Real ID, or costs of tests though the DMV. 950 SE 3rd Street Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 2pm-6pm, Tuesday-Thursday 10am-2pm Email (541) 389-6643 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL LA PINE SOCIAL SERVICES OFFICE SOCIAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency food boxes, clothing for adults, household items (kitchenware and small appliances), prescription assistance, gas money (for job interviews or appointments), emergency motel vouchers, and ID assistance. 51484 Morson Road La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-noon and 12:30pm-2pm Email (541) 536-6135 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL MEDFORD SOCIAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides personal care supplies such as toothpaste and brushes, shampoo and conditioner, feminine products, deodorant, soap, and bus transportation passes. Helps with obtaining an Oregon ID or driver's license and is once per lifetime. All services are based on funding and current availability. 2424 N Pacific Highway Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 11am-1pm Email (541) 772-3828 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center with laundry, showers, internet access, mail services, clothing vouchers, on-site clothing, and hygiene supplies. Assistance in obtaining identification documents. 4060 W Amazon Drive Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with children under age 18 and pregnant women (proof of pregnancy required) who are experiencing homelessness Hours: 7 days per week 9am-5pm (541) 342-7728 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS IDENTIFICATION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a Determination of Homelessness form needed to apply for an ID Card from the DMV. The agency does not provide drivers licenses or birth certificates. 650 NW Irving Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday-Sunday 8:30am-3pm (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSPONDER RESOURCE NAVIGATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral resources including healthcare, insurance, social services, housing, and mental health. Offers an online directory of trans-inclusive local resources, covering everything from healthcare and bodywork to faith services and crisis support. Assists with name/gender legal document change through the courts or at the DMV, with services ranging from providing information about changing documents in Oregon to offering financial aid for document fees. The level of financial assistance and eligibility for this assistance varies based on the program's budget. The message line is staffed by volunteers and it may take a few days for calls to be returned. 440 Maxwell Road Eugene, OR 97404 Eligibility: Individuals who are transgender or gender-diverse Hours: By appointment only Email (541) 321-0872 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VINA MOSES CENTER VINA'S AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help with rent, utilities (electric, natural gas, oil, propane, wood and water), prescriptions, identification cards, and other urgent needs. Usually only able to pay a portion of the requested amount. Funding is limited and assistance is not guaranteed. Can provide referrals to other agencies for further needs. 968 NW Garfield Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 752-4688 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WHITE BIRD CLINIC BIRTH CERTIFICATES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with obtaining State of Oregon birth certificates. 323 E 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 9:30am-12:30pm Email (541) 342-1295 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM BASIC NEEDS LGBTQ2+ HEALTH CARE ONE COMMUNITY HEALTH LGBTQ2+ HEALTH CARE OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES IDENTIFICATION CARD FEE WAIVER OUTSIDE IN ID PROJECT PDX SAINTS LOVE DAY CENTER POLK COUNTY RESOURCE CENTERS RESOURCE CENTER PROJECT HOMELESS CONNECT IDENTIFICATION ASSISTANCE SOCIETY OF ST VINCENT DE PAUL SOCIAL SERVICES OF BEND ID AND DRIVERS LICENSE ASSISTANCE ST VINCENT DE PAUL LA PINE SOCIAL SERVICES OFFICE SOCIAL SERVICES ST VINCENT DE PAUL MEDFORD SOCIAL SERVICES ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER TRANSITION PROJECTS IDENTIFICATION ASSISTANCE TRANSPONDER
